title: EquiGrass™ 5kg Professional Grass Seed Repair Mix for Paddocks and Grazing Areas, Covering ½ Acre
features: Repair: Our grass seed mix has been designed to restore grass back to a pristine, healthy condition.
description: Product Description EquiGrass is a specialist range of lawn seeds designed especially for growing grass in equine areas such as paddocks and grazing fields. Our professional EquiGrass range features grass seeds carefully designed to meet the requirements of the equine industry. Lawns Direct has spent many years researching and developing innovative lawn seeds and our passion for all things gardening is what motivates us to create products that are affordable without sacrificing the exceptional, premium quality. In equine areas, like paddocks, horse fields and stables, there are often problems with grass becoming patchy. This is because, as well as being subject to ever-changing weather conditions, there are also horses grazing on this on a daily basis. It’s important to use a seed that is quick to establish so that food and nutrients are always readily available for your horses and ponies. Not sure where to start when it comes to planting a lawn? We have put together some useful advice on how to prepare. It’s essential to sow your seeds at the right time of year if you want to create a healthy lawn in your equine area. Grass seeds should be planted when temperatures sit between 8-10° and there have been at least 14 consecutive days without rain. These two things will affect how well grass grows so it is important to think about them before starting the process. Make sure you take the time to prepare your soil by removing weeds and debris, and levelling the surface to ensure healthy growth. Preparation is hugely important when it comes to sowing a new lawn or restoring patches in your equine area. When preparing, you should remove any weeds, debris and foliage, rake and level the ground and treat with fertiliser. It’s also important to rotate the soil to at least 10cm deep using an efficient tool like the eSkde Garden Cultivator for smaller areas or a Tractor for larger areas. Spread seeds evenly either by hand or using a gardening tool to ensure consistent growth across the lawn. Start by distributing seeds out across an even seabed. You should aim for a rate of 50mg per m2, and do this step by hand or with a seed spreader to achieve even coverage. Once this is done, it’s important to mix the seeds into the soil so that they have the best chance of growing into a healthy lawn. Finish off by firming the soil either by hand or using a roller. Aftercare is one of the most important steps in establishing a new lawn and should be carried out to ensure your lawn always looks its best. It’s not just about the preparation and sowing but the aftercare too. During the first 6 weeks, you should water generously, mowing each time the grass reaches 5-7cm in length and fertilise the area well. Preparation Sowing Watering Aftercare Want to hear a cool fact about horses? Quality grassland is essential for year-round grazing because good quality grazing pasture is the healthiest and most natural diet for horses. Lawns Direct has created the EquiGrass range to make life easier for horse owners, whether you manage a paddock or have a private field. Our team is here to help you get the very best grass seed to restore your grass back to a beautiful, healthy condition. The EquiGrass range offers everything you need to restore grass in your paddocks, stables, and horse fields while providing a reliable and consistent source of food for your animals. Highly Effective Quick Establishing Equine Grass Seed Mix for Paddocks, Fields & Other Grazing Areas There are many reasons that customers choose to use our specialist equine grass seeds, including our understanding of horse nutrition, highly rated customer feedback and fast, reliable delivery. Our Professional EquiGrass Grass Seed Repair Mix is ideal for areas where your horses are regularly grazing which is resulting in patchy areas of grass. This product uses a blend of Perennial Ryegrass which is a hardy and productive species, principally grown for hay, haylage, silage, and grazing. The quick establishing nature of these seeds means that you can maintain a consistent food source for your horses and as well as having highly resistant pristine-looking grazing area all year round! Restores grazing areas to a healthy and pristine looking grass. Quick establishment for a food source all year round. Completely safe for consumption by horses and ponies. Highly resistant to harsh weather conditions like snow, ice and rain. ProGrass products are designed for commercial and domestic lawns such as private gardens and community parks. These high-quality seeds can be used in areas with very little sunlight and still grow into healthy grass. The SportsGrass range offers resilient, durable options for high impact areas such as rugby pitches, football fields and more. After researching and testing our seeds in sports areas, we decided to create our SportsGrass range. It offers low maintenance, and durability to create a resilient turf that is able to withstand the impact associated with sports. Our EquiGrass range has been developed with horse-owners in mind and can be safely used in paddocks, stables and fields. The EquiGrass range of seeds is quick to establish, which is ideal in equine areas where horses are grazing and there needs to be a consistent supply of food. These seeds are packed with nutrition and are completely safe for your livestock to eat.
